title: Sanjaya Mendis
content:
People.ContactEmail smendis@mccarthy.ca
- University of Ottawa
Sanjaya Mendis is a leading intellectual property litigator and a trusted advocate for clients navigating complex patent disputes. A partner in our award-winning IP Litigation Group—recognized as Canadian Patent Litigation Firm of the Year by LMG Life Sciences for two consecutive years and consistently ranked Band 1 by Chambers —Sanjaya delivers strategic, results-driven counsel in high-stakes litigation.
With a focus on life sciences and technology, Sanjaya has built a reputation for successfully handling some of Canada’s most significant patent cases. He has deep expertise in biologic and biosimilar litigation, having acted in major mandates for Regeneron/Bayer, AbbVie, Roche/Genentech, and Moderna. His practice spans over 40 reported decisions across the Federal Court, Federal Court of Appeal, and the Supreme Court of Canada—reflecting both his litigation acumen and his commitment to achieving meaningful outcomes for clients.
Sanjaya is frequently called upon to coordinate multi-jurisdictional patent disputes, offering seamless cross-border strategy and execution. Clients value his clarity, responsiveness, and unwavering focus on their business goals. Sanjaya has acted as lead or co-lead counsel in some of Canada’s most high-profile and complex patent litigation matters. His experience spans blockbuster biologics, cutting-edge vaccines, and strategic patent advisory roles for global innovators:
- Regeneron and Bayer – Co-lead counsel in multiple ongoing biosimilar patent infringement actions involving EYLEA® (aflibercept), a leading ophthalmic biologic.
- Hoffmann-La Roche and Genentech – Co-lead counsel in a biosimilar patent infringement action involving PERJETA® (pertuzumab), a blockbuster breast cancer treatment.
- Moderna – Retained to defend Moderna in the first-ever Canadian patent litigation involving its COVID-19 vaccine, initiated by Genevant.
- Merck – Co-lead counsel in two ongoing patent infringement actions involving BRIDION , a key surgical medicine.
- AbbVie – Trial counsel in AbbVie’s successful biosimilar patent infringement action involving HUMIRA® , one of the world’s most valuable biologics: AbbVie Corporation v. Jamp Pharma Corporation , 2023 FC 1520 (appeal ongoing).
- Merck – Trial counsel in a successful patent infringement action against Pharmascience involving JANUVIA® , JANUMET® , and JANUMET® XR (sitagliptin): Merck Sharp & Dohme Corp. v. Pharmascience Inc. , 2022 FC 417 .
- BMS and Pfizer – Trial and appellate counsel in a successful patent infringement action involving ELIQUIS® (apixaban), resulting in upheld patent validity and dismissal of appeal: BMS v. Pharmascience and Sandoz , 2021 FC 1 ; Pharmascience Inc. v. BMS , 2022 FCA 142 .
- Apple – Counsel in a confidential patent infringement action brought by Wi-LAN involving Apple’s iPhones, iPads, and Watches (settled pre-trial).
Sanjaya as been recognized by numerous leading ranking publications, including:
- Legal 500 client testimonial: “Sanjaya Mendis excels in patent disputes” (2025)
- LMG Life Science’s Star (2025)
- Managing IP Rising Star (2022-2025)
Beyond the courtroom, Sanjaya is a recognized thought leader in patent law. He has been invited to speak at premier forums including the Fordham IP Conference in New York and the University of Toronto Patent Colloquium. Earlier this year, Sanjaya spoke about vaccine patent litigation at the Pharmaceutical & Biotech Patent Litigation Summit in Amsterdam. This past fall, he additionally discussed Canada’s patent linkage regime at the North American Patent Litigation Conference in Philadelphia.
